question_text:
Which of the following is true of a 
monopolistically competitive firm in 
long-run equilibrium?

options.A:
It produces where price equals marginal 
cost, and it earns zero economic profits.
options.B:
It produces where marginal revenue exceeds 
marginal cost, and it earns positive 
economic profits.
options.C:
It produces where marginal cost equals 
marginal revenue, and the price is equal to 
average total cost.
options.D:
It produces at the minimum average total 
cost, and it utilizes all excess capacity.
options.E:
It produces more than the allocatively 
efficient quantity, and the price is greater 
than marginal cost.

question_text:

the price of haircuts decreases?

will not change.

The marginal product of the last barber hired 
will decrease.
options.C:

to the right.
options.D:
The marginal revenue product curve will shift 
to the left.
options.E:
There will be a movement upward along 
the marginal revenue curve.

question_text:

dominant strategy will do which of the following?

options.A:

strategies chosen by its competitor.
options.B:
Adjust its strategies based on the strategies 
chosen by its competitor.
options.C:
Make the first move, and wait to see whether 
its competitor responds to the move.
options.D:
Keep its competitor guessing about its 
next move.
options.E:
Collude with its competitor to reduce 
uncertainty.
